summaries.value: Anonymous Middle English prose translation, probably composed in the first half of the fourteenth century, of a glossed Gallican Psalter and appended canticles and Athanasian Creed. Linguistic evidence indicates that the English rendering was made at least partly through a French intermediary. It survives complete in four manuscripts: BL Add MS 17376, TCD MS 69, Princeton Scheide MS 143, and Cambridge, Magdalene College, Pepys MS 2498. The work is not securely the earliest complete Middle English prose Psalter, is not by William of Shoreham, and cannot be localized to London merely because the surviving witnesses' scribal language has been localized there.

The cataloged work is the complete 162-part Middle English prose translation: 150 Psalms, eleven canticles, and the Athanasian Creed. Bülbring’s preliminary matter, errata, residual page scaffolding, Latin source layer, manuscript non-target contents, and later scholarship are outside the intellectual-work boundary.

All four complete English witnesses transmit Latin verse and glosses with the English translation, but the repository target serializes Bülbring’s English edition. The French mediation is supported at least in part; BnF Français 6260 is collateral and not the translator’s physical exemplar.

extent.description: One prose work divided into 162 cataloged components: 150 Psalms, eleven canticles, and the Athanasian Creed. All 162 repository body divisions are represented once; verse metrics are zero because the target is prose.

content_parts.label: Psalm 1
content_parts.summary: Psalm 1. Incipit: Blesced be þe man, þat ȝede nouȝt in þe counseil of wicked, ne stode nouȝt in þe waie of sinȝeres, ne sat nauȝt in fals iugement. Explicit: For oure Lord knew þe waie of þe ryȝtful, and þe waye of synners schal perissen.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=8, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=11.

content_parts.label: Psalm 2
content_parts.summary: Psalm 2. Incipit: Whi douteþ hij hem of þe lawe, þe folk wyþ-outen lawe, & folk þoȝt idel þynges? Explicit: Whan he be styred in hys short ire, blisced ben hij, þat afien in hym.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=13, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=15.

content_parts.label: Psalm 3
content_parts.summary: Psalm 3. Incipit: Lord, why ben hij multiplied þat trublen me? mani arisen aȝeins me. Explicit: Helþe ys of oure Lord, and þy blisseing, Lord, hys on þy folk.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=8, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=10.

content_parts.label: Psalm 4
content_parts.summary: Psalm 4. Incipit: As ich cleped, God of my ryȝt herd me; þou, Lord, forbare me in my tribulacioun. Explicit: For þou, Lord, haþ on-liche stablist me in hope.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=10, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=15.

content_parts.label: Psalm 5
content_parts.summary: Psalm 5. Incipit: Lord, take myn wordes wyþ þyne eren; vnderstonde my crye. Explicit: Lord, þou hast crouned us as wyþ þe shelde of þy gode wylle.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=15, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=16.

content_parts.label: Psalm 6
content_parts.summary: Psalm 6. Incipit: Lord, ne repruce me nouȝt in þy vengeaunce; ne reproue me nouȝt in þyn yre. Explicit: Wax alle myn enemys asshamed, and ben hij greteliche trubled; ben hij conuerted, & shame hij ful swyftlich.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=10, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=16.

content_parts.label: Psalm 7
content_parts.summary: Psalm 7. Incipit: Lord, my God, ich hoped in þe; make me saufe of alle þat pursuen me, & deliuer me fram alle yuel; Explicit: Ich shal shryue to our Lord after hys ryȝtfulnesse, and synge to þe name of þe heȝest Lord.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=18, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=25.

content_parts.label: Psalm 8
content_parts.summary: Psalm 8. Incipit: Ha þou, Lord, our Lord, ful wonderful hys þy name in al þerþe. Explicit: Ha Lord, our Lord, ful wonderful ys þy name in alle erþe.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=9, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=11.

content_parts.label: Psalm 9
content_parts.summary: Psalm 9. Incipit: Ich shal shryue to þe, Lord, in alle myn hert; ich shal tellen al þyn wonders. Explicit: To iuge þe moderles and þe meke, þat man sett nouȝt to herien hem vp þerþe.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=41, PB=3, MILESTONE=7, NOTE1=66, NOTE2=1.

content_parts.label: Psalm 10
content_parts.summary: Psalm 10. Incipit: Ich affie me in our Lord; hou saie ȝe wicked to mi soule, Wende þou in-to heuen as a sparwe? Explicit: For our Lord ys riȝtful, ande he loueþ riȝtfulnes; hys semblaun saiȝ euennis.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=8, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=9.

content_parts.label: Psalm 11
content_parts.summary: Psalm 11. Incipit: Ha Lord, make me sauf, for þe holi failed in parfit holynes; for soþnes ben litteled fram mennes sones. Explicit: Þe wicked ȝeden abouten, þou multiplidest mennes sones efter þyn eȝenes.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=9, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=13.

content_parts.label: Psalm 12
content_parts.summary: Psalm 12. Incipit: Ha Sir, hou longe forȝetestou me on ende? hou longe turnestou þy face fram me? Explicit: Min hert shal ioien in þyn helþe; ich shal singe to our Lord, þat ȝaf to me godes, and y shal synge to þe name of our Lord alderȝeste (!).
notes: XML descendant counts: P=6, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=9.

content_parts.label: Psalm 13
content_parts.summary: Psalm 13. Incipit: Þe vnwyse seid in his hert, It nys God. Explicit: Who shal ȝyf fram þe heuen helþe to Israel? whan our Lord haþ turned oway þe chaytifnesse of hijs folk, þe kynreden of Iakob shal gladen, and þe folk of Israel shal ioyen.

content_parts.label: Psalm 14
content_parts.summary: Psalm 14. Incipit: Lord, who shal wonen in þy tabernacle, oþer who shal resten in þyn holy hill? Explicit: He þat doþ þes þynges, ne shal nouȝt be stired wyþ-outen ende.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=7, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=11.

content_parts.label: Psalm 15
content_parts.summary: Psalm 15. Incipit: Kepe me, Lord, for ich hoped in þe; ich seide to our Lord, Þou art my God, for þou ne hast no nede of myn godes. Explicit: Þou madest knowen to me þe waies of lyf, þou shalt fulfillen me of ioie wyþ [þy] face; delitynges ben in þy riȝt honde vnto þe ende.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=11, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=16.

content_parts.label: Psalm 16
content_parts.summary: Psalm 16. Incipit: Here, Lord, my riȝt; vnderstonde my praier. Explicit: And y shal apperen in ryȝt to þy siȝt; y shal be fild, whan þy glorie haþ shewed.

content_parts.label: Psalm 17
content_parts.summary: Psalm 17. Incipit: Ha Lord, y shal loue þe, my strengþe, my fasteninge, my refut, and my deliuerer of iuel. Explicit: Heriand þe helþes of his kynge and doand mercy to hys Dauid anoit wyþ creme and hys sede vnto þe world.

notes: XML descendant counts: P=16, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=20, NOTE2=1.

content_parts.label: Psalm 19
content_parts.summary: Psalm 19. Incipit: Ha þou my soule, her our Lord þe in þe day of þy tribulacioun; þe name of þe God Jacob defende þe fram iuel. Explicit: Lord, make þou þe kyng sauf, and her us in þe daie þat we haue cleped þe.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=10, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=8.

content_parts.label: Psalm 20
content_parts.summary: Psalm 20. Incipit: Lord, þe kyng shal gladen in þy vertu, and he shal greteleche ioyen vp þyn helþe. Explicit: Be þou, Lord, an-heȝed in þy vertu; we shul syngen & psalmen þy vertuȝ.

content_parts.label: Psalm 21
content_parts.summary: Psalm 21. Incipit: Ha God, my God, loke in me; whi for-soke þou me? þe wordes of min trespases ben fer fram myn helþe. Explicit: Þe kynde þat hys to comen shal be shewed to our Lord, and þe heuens shul shewe hys riȝtfulnes to þe folk þat shal ben borne, which our Lord made.
notes: XML descendant counts: P=33, PB=2, MILESTONE=5, NOTE1=40.

content_parts.summary: Psalm 22. Incipit: Our Lord gouerneþ me, and noþyng shal defailen to me; in þe stede of pasture he sett me þer. Explicit: And þat ich wonne in þe hous of our Lord in lengþe of daies.

content_parts.summary: Psalm 23. Incipit: Þe erþe is our Lordes and his plente, þe world and ichon þat woneþ þer-inne. Explicit: Which is he, þat kynge of glorie? Þe Lord of uertu, he his kynge of glorie.

notes: All four complete English witnesses preserve a Latin verse and glosses followed by the Middle English translation; the repository target serializes Bülbring's English edition, not the complete Latin layer.
Pepys may have independent priority within a lost-archetype stemma proposed by Black and St-Jacques, but it is not promoted as the original translation.
The target includes the appended eleven canticles and Athanasian Creed, not merely the 150 Psalms.
The sole work-language statement is Middle English (enm); Latin and French belong to source/witness layers.
The preferred title, anonymous translator, cautious date, broad England origin, prose form, and complete 162-part structure are kept distinct from edition and witness metadata.
